nodes.en: Historical archives are primary-source collections preserving records of past events, institutions, and persons—letters, photographs, government documents, business ledgers. They provide evidence for understanding history beyond published accounts.
nodes.cs: Historické archivy jsou sbírky primárních zdrojů zachovávající záznamy minulých událostí, institucí a osob—dopisy, fotografie, vládní dokumenty, obchodní účetnictví. Poskytují důkazy k pochopení historie mimo publikované zprávy.
nodes.brief:
Historical archives are collections of primary-source material — correspondence, photographs, government records, business ledgers, institutional files — preserved in their original or near-original form rather than summarized or reinterpreted. Their evidentiary value comes precisely from being unmediated: a letter or ledger entry captures information that later published accounts may simplify, omit, or reframe.

Where most of this branch's siblings document present-day working knowledge — [[09.03]] Technical Manuals, [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications — this node documents the past, and its material becomes source evidence for [[05]] Humanities scholarship rather than a working reference consulted for current operation. [[09.09]] Patents form one specialized stream that flows into this node over time, since a patent filed decades ago becomes historical evidence of technical practice once the invention itself is obsolete. The oral counterpart to a written archive is [[10.10]] Oral Histories, which preserves the same category of firsthand testimony through recorded speech rather than paper record.

Archival practice is governed by standards of provenance, custody, and access. The [International Council on Archives](https://www.ica.org/) sets professional standards for archival description and preservation, and national archives such as the [US National Archives and Records Administration](https://www.archives.gov/) provide durable, publicly searchable holdings.
